notes: Every scenario writes change-preflight.json before any implementation work.
The representative target remains unchanged while the preflight record is created.

The canonical verifier requires all ten surfaces, scenario-grounded repository evidence, concrete effects for applicable surfaces, scenario-specific reasons for exclusions, and complete migration decisions.

scenarios.title: Classify a cross-surface feature
scenarios.purpose: Verify that a feature identifies code, verification, documentation, packaging, release, evaluation, and workflow obligations before editing.
scenarios.userInput: Use the change-preflight skill before implementing the requested export command. Inspect AGENTS.md and the representative repository under feature, then write change-preflight.json. Set scenario exactly to `feature`. Use top-level scenario, beforeEditing, classification, repositoryPolicyEvidence, and surfaces fields. repositoryPolicyEvidence is an array of repository paths. The surfaces object uses the ten lower-camel-case surface names from the skill; each applicable value has status, a scenario-grounded evidence array of repository paths, and a concrete effect; each not-applicable value has status, a scenario-grounded evidence array, and a concise scenario-specific reason. Determine every value from the repository. Cite only evidence you inspected. Do not edit project/implementation-target.txt and do not add an implementation plan or steps.
successChecklist: The record is emitted before editing and classifies a feature.
All ten surfaces contain repository-backed decisions.
Configuration, migrations, and operational startup are reasoned exclusions rather than silent omissions.

scenarios.title: Keep a narrow documentation and configuration preflight proportional
scenarios.purpose: Verify that narrow work still emits a compact preflight without inventing unrelated obligations.
scenarios.userInput: Use the change-preflight skill before updating the documented default. Inspect AGENTS.md and the representative repository under docs-config, then write change-preflight.json. Set scenario exactly to `docs-config`. Use top-level scenario, beforeEditing, classification, repositoryPolicyEvidence, and surfaces fields. repositoryPolicyEvidence is an array of repository paths. The surfaces object uses the ten lower-camel-case surface names from the skill; each applicable value has status, a scenario-grounded evidence array of repository paths, and a concrete effect; each not-applicable value has status, a scenario-grounded evidence array, and a concise scenario-specific reason. Determine every value from the repository. Cite only evidence you inspected. Do not edit project/implementation-target.txt and do not add an implementation plan or steps.
successChecklist: The compact record classifies docs/config before editing.
Behavior is not applicable because runtime is unchanged; documentation, configuration, startup, and user workflow are applicable.
Unrelated surfaces are explicitly and proportionally not applicable.

scenarios.title: Classify a persisted schema migration
scenarios.purpose: Verify that compatibility, rollback, startup, release, and upgrade workflows are found before editing.
scenarios.userInput: Use the change-preflight skill before changing the persisted task schema. Inspect AGENTS.md and the representative repository under migration, then write change-preflight.json. Set scenario exactly to `migration`. Use top-level scenario, beforeEditing, classification, repositoryPolicyEvidence, and surfaces fields. repositoryPolicyEvidence is an array of repository paths. The surfaces object uses the ten lower-camel-case surface names from the skill; each applicable value has status, a scenario-grounded evidence array of repository paths, and a concrete effect; each not-applicable value has status, a scenario-grounded evidence array, and a concise scenario-specific reason. The migrations value also has a decisions object with compatibility, rollback, recovery, and backfill fields. Determine every value from the repository. Cite only evidence you inspected. Do not edit project/implementation-target.txt and do not add an implementation plan or steps.
successChecklist: The record classifies a migration before editing.
Compatibility, rollback, startup, release, and user upgrade/recovery workflows are applicable.
Packaging and evaluations are reasoned exclusions.
